articles.title: WITH ASANA
articles.summary: Marketers everywhere are feeling the pressure: launch more campaigns, ship them faster, do it all with fewer resources—and prove that you're actually moving the needle on revenue.

articles.title: WHEN CONSUMERS ARE FINANCIALLY STRESSED, THEY LOOK FOR RELIABILITY
articles.summary: Financial stress is reshaping how consumers evaluate brands. A recent study found that only 52% of consumers feel financially secure, and those who feel insecure are more risk-averse and less tolerant of friction. These consumers rate brand experiences lower on satisfaction and trust, prioritizing reliability and recommendations over price. In uncertain economic conditions, trust reduces decision fatigue and becomes a key driver of loyalty.

articles.title: AT CES 2026, MARKETERS GET SERIOUS ABOUT AGENTIC AI, CREATORS, AND RETAIL MEDIA
articles.summary: CES 2026 showed a shift from flashy technology to practical execution in marketing. Agentic AI gained momentum as it moves toward automated media buying while requiring trust and transparency. Creators are evolving into full media businesses as they expand into streaming and podcasting and gain pricing power. SiriusXM saw a 300% increase in podcast revenue as creators adopted omnichannel distribution and programmatic access. Retail media growth now depends on off-site investment and consistent measurement, as return on ad spend (ROAS) varies by as much as 63% across networks.

articles.title: VENMO MARKETING STRATEGY: GROWTH TACTICS ON HOW THEY MADE P2P PAYMENTS SOCIAL
articles.summary: Venmo grew by making payments social, so the interaction mattered more than the transaction. It targeted millennials and Gen Z, ages 18 to 40, and positioned itself as a social payment app built around a feed and emojis. Visible transactions created built-in virality. Each payment acted as a micro-advertisement. The brand's scale and cultural relevance are reinforced by its influencer campaigns and partnerships on social platforms.

articles.title: THE STEP-BY-STEP PROCESS BEHIND OUR 2025 WRAPPED EMAIL
articles.summary: EmailLove's 2025 Wrapped email followed a 7-step process. It started by auditing data and defining an active-user segment, then cleaning and uploading it as custom fields in MailerLite. The email was designed, merge tags were tested, and it was sent to around 1,000 users. It reached a 57% open rate with no unsubscribes or spam complaints, but generated no clicks. Adding CTAs, share options, personality labels, or interactive elements could turn passive stats into engagement.

articles.title: HOW TO CONVERT FREE USERS INTO PAYING SUBSCRIBERS
TIRED OF CAMPAIGN CHAOS? STAY ON TRACK WITH ASANA
articles.summary: Design in one application, copy in another, action items in a spreadsheet—is it any wonder that things fall between the cracks? Asana [10] lets you see the full picture across campaigns, review and approve assets in one place, and keep everyone informed. Try Asana for

articles.title: WRITESONIC
articles.summary: Writesonic tracks how a brand appears in AI search and identifies gaps where competitors are favored. It shows which content is cited and suggests updates before visibility drops. The platform generates content that ranks on AI and Google and provides data on search trends and competitor strategies. Everything is designed to guide precise actions that increase reach and influence without relying on guesswork.

articles.title: GMAIL IS ENTERING THE GEMINI ERA
articles.summary: Gmail is moving deeper into AI assistance with the launch of Gemini-powered features designed to reduce inbox friction. AI Overviews summarize long threads and answer natural-language questions by synthesizing information across years of emails, removing the need for keyword searches or manual scanning. At the same time, writing tools like Help Me Write and upgraded Suggested Replies focus on speed and tone alignment, positioning Gmail as an active writing collaborator.

articles.title: AI WRITES BRILLIANTLY
articles.summary: AI writes well and can match a writer's voice, but it also creates too many strong versions. Combining them becomes a slow, clumsy process that often feels worse than starting fresh. The unused lines make it easy to doubt every choice. The real lesson is simple. Pick one good draft, call it done, and move on.

articles.title: WE BUILT HELL, THEN ASKED FOR A DISCOUNT
articles.summary: Humans want fast and high-quality experiences. They create systems that deliver short-term ease while ignoring long-term tradeoffs. When friction or failure appears, they blame others instead of recognizing the choices that caused it. This cycle repeats across industries and history, from health insurance and travel to government and social media. People end up paying a misery premium for experiences they designed themselves, and the pattern shows no sign of stopping.
